chief_complaint: A 65-year-old man with myelodysplastic syndrome and monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined significance developed acquired pure red cell aplasia 2 weeks after receiving the BNT162b2 mRNA COVID-19 vaccine.
history: The patient developed acquired pure red cell aplasia (aPRCA) 2 weeks after receiving the BNT162b2 mRNA coronavirus disease 2019 vaccine, with Epstein-Barr virus DNA detected at the onset. The patient did not respond to immunosuppressive therapy and required red blood cell transfusion every one to two weeks.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: Epstein-Barr virus DNA detected, red blood cell count significantly reduced.
correct_diagnosis: Acquired Pure Red Cell Aplasia Following BNT162b2 mRNA COVID-19 Vaccination
differentials: Acquired Pure Red Cell Aplasia Following BNT162b2 mRNA COVID-19 Vaccination
correct_ranking: Acquired Pure Red Cell Aplasia Following BNT162b2 mRNA COVID-19 Vaccination
